history.text: Impatience with actions, patience with results.
history.author: Naval Ravikant

history.text: I have already lost touch with a couple of people I used to be.
history.author: Joan Didion

history.text: I’m the gap between what I’d like to be and what others have made of me.
history.author: Fernando Pessoa

history.text: The life you want is being built in the quiet, unphotogenic minutes of the Monday you’re currently surviving.
history.author: Original

history.text: You didn’t outgrow your childhood home; you just stopped liking the version of yourself you had to play to survive the l
history.author: Original

history.text: The loneliest red flag is when you start apologizing for the look on your face the moment they hurt you.
history.author: Original

history.text: Your assumptions are your windows on the world. Scrub them off every once in a while.
history.author: Isaac Asimov

history.text: The future enters into us, in order to transform itself in us long before it happens.
history.author: Rainer Maria Rilke

history.text: There is a quiet panic in noticing your mother’s new medicines while you are still waiting for the life you promised her
history.author: Original

history.text: We are the ‘good kids’ who grew up to be successful strangers, inhabiting lives bought by the parts of us that didn’t su
history.author: Original

history.text: The biggest scam you ever fell for was thinking you had to 'feel like it' to start.
history.author: Original

history.text: A messy, 20% effort day beats a perfect day spent worrying about being behind.
history.author: Original

history.text: I’ve always found that the most beautiful things happen when you stop trying to control the outcome.
history.author: Irrfan Khan

history.text: Anyone can give you attention when you're interesting. Only a few will give you care when you're exhausting.
history.author: Original

history.text: The ability to observe without evaluating is the highest form of intelligence.” — Jiddu Krishnamurti
history.author: Original

history.text: At some point in life the world's beauty becomes enough. You don't need to photograph, paint or even remember it. It is
history.author: Toni Morrison
